canonical_name: Crucible Plan 88 Custos Plan 18 Task 2 requirements-only consumer review

consumer.authority_owned: StrategyRelease lifecycle and immutable accepted release snapshot

producer_receipt.note: This consumer review supplies the missing Crucible requirements decision; it does not rewrite or promote the producer receipt.

engine_binding.note: These are Nautilus execution-extra requirements and do not raise the Custos root contracts package baseline.

canonicalization.model_digest_input: strict parsed JSON with duplicate keys and non-finite numbers rejected
canonicalization.encoding: UTF-8 compact JSON with recursively sorted object keys and exact decimal rendering
canonicalization.consumer_rule: Crucible consumes and verifies producer digests; it does not introduce a second BOM canonicalization API.

compatibility_checks.evidence: Custos owns execution ABI and artifact-verification schemas; Crucible owns StrategyRelease; Philosophers-Stone owns canonical release BOM production.
All six schemas forbid unknown properties and pin schema_version 1.
Command and execution context require deployment instance, immutable spec id/digest, effective config digest and generation >= 1.
Artifact reference, complete BOM projection, attestation, SBOM, contract schema, local trust policy and loaded entry point remain lossless.
DevelopmentSourceRefV1 is sandbox-only and non-promotable; live/testnet require artifact binding.
Manifest pins alephain.strategy_runtime.v1 and Nautilus 1.230.0, requires an importable module:attribute, and rejects the legacy shared and pandas_ta top-level aliases.
The Rust consumer preserves the full command, release BOM, artifact and receipt fields and rejects unknown fields, but does not yet enforce every producer const, pattern or cross-field validator.
The golden demonstrates field mapping with synthetic digest values; it is not a real wheel, loader, attestation or runtime acceptance receipt.

current_consumer_gaps: StrategyManifestV1 Rust deserialization does not yet enforce execution_abi, entry_point_group, engine, engine_version, requires_python or entry_point syntax and forbidden aliases.
DevelopmentSourceRefV1 Rust deserialization currently permits testnet/live trading modes and promotable=true although the producer schema requires sandbox and false.
StrategyArtifactRefV1 Rust validation checks artifact_kind only for non-empty text rather than the wheel constant, and attestation const/pattern/uniqueness rules are not yet complete.
StrategyExecutionCommandBindingV1 Rust deserialization does not independently enforce generation >= 1, non-empty complete BOM projection or ArtifactRef digest cross-links.

StrategyArtifactVerificationReceiptV1 Rust deserialization does not yet recompute the canonical artifact_ref_digest, require verified_members to equal the command BOM, match local trust policy to attestation, or validate the loaded entry point.
Crucible has not yet implemented or verified sha256-canonical-json-v1 for producer model-digest checks; it must consume the producer algorithm without creating a second BOM authority.

producer_handoff.requested_action: Vendor this exact consumer review into the canonical Custos Plan 18 Task 2 receipt chain and bind its byte digest together with producer commit b36e9edf3ce9d2080e0d77b22ae99a65e32aaaf0 and asset-index digest d87d6fc2df020e92748058c5577863b83dd6f3b2a0c0f59adbf9b9b7822dae07.
does_not_mean: Custos Plan 18 Task 2 runtime verification is complete
the Custos producer receipt is handoff READY
Crucible Plan 88 is READY or complete
the Python lifecycle golden is accepted runtime evidence

runtime_followups: Custos publishes its canonical Plan 18 Task 2 receipt with the exact producer commit, asset-index digest and vendored consumer-review digest.
Crucible closes every typed-consumer const, pattern, uniqueness, canonical-digest and cross-field validation gap listed above.
Crucible runs the focused Rust golden/negative checks and authority byte-drift gate in the normal Plan 88 execution workflow.
Plan 88 persists the exact accepted StrategyRelease artifact snapshot and proves attestation, idempotency and outbox atomicity.
Philosophers-Stone Plan 54 supplies a real immutable release BOM and artifact set before real-artifact acceptance.
Custos verifies a real wheel, resolves and loads the manifest entry point under the pinned ABI, and emits a real StrategyArtifactVerificationReceiptV1.
Only the later runtime consumer receipt may satisfy the Plan 88 READY gate.
